senators.name: Mark Kelly
senators.nerdReason: B.S. in marine engineering and nautical science from U.S. Merchant Marine Academy. M.S. in aeronautical engineering from U.S. Naval Postgraduate School. NASA astronaut who commanded two Space Shuttle missions and flew four missions total, logging 50+ days in space. Navy combat pilot with 39 combat missions and 5,000+ flight hours. One of the most technically credentialed members of the U.S. Senate.
education: M.S. Aeronautical Engineering, U.S. Naval Postgraduate School
B.S. Marine Engineering and Nautical Science, U.S. Merchant Marine Academy

senators.name: Ruben Gallego
senators.notNerdReason: B.A. in international relations from Harvard. Marine Corps combat veteran (Iraq). Career in politics and military. No STEM or technical background. Strong academic credentials but focused on political science and foreign affairs rather than evidence-based or data-driven governance.

AZ-01.name: David Schweikert
AZ-01.notNerdReason: B.S. and M.B.A. from Arizona State University. Real estate and financial background. Known for interest in technology and innovation policy, frequently discusses fintech and biotech on the House floor. However, no STEM degree and engagement with technology is more advocacy than application.

AZ-02.name: Eli Crane
AZ-02.notNerdReason: Attended Arizona Western College and University of Arizona but did not graduate. Career as a Navy SEAL and small business owner. No academic credentials or STEM background. No notable evidence-based policy focus.

AZ-03.name: Yassamin Ansari
AZ-03.nerdReason: B.A. in international relations from Stanford and M.Phil. from Cambridge (Gates Scholar). Worked for UN Secretary-General on Paris Agreement climate policy. Exceptionally strong academic credentials and deep policy engagement, but degrees are in political science/international relations rather than STEM. Climate policy work is evidence-informed but background is diplomatic rather than scientific.
education: M.Phil. International Relations and Politics, University of Cambridge (Gates Scholar)
experience: Senior Policy Advisor to UN Secretary-General

AZ-04.name: Greg Stanton
AZ-04.notNerdReason: B.A. in history and political science from Marquette (magna cum laude, Phi Beta Kappa) and J.D. from University of Michigan. Career in law and city government. Strong academic credentials but no STEM or technical background.
education: B.A. History and Political Science, Marquette University (magna cum laude, Phi Beta Kappa)

AZ-05.name: Andy Biggs
AZ-05.notNerdReason: B.A. in Asian studies, J.D., and M.A. in political science. Career in law and state politics. No STEM background or evidence-based policy focus. Degrees span humanities and law.
education: M.A. Political Science, Arizona State University
B.A. Asian Studies, Brigham Young University

AZ-06.name: Juan Ciscomani
AZ-06.notNerdReason: B.A. from University of Arizona. Career in community outreach, business advocacy, and government advising. No STEM or technical background. First-generation college graduate with a public service career path.
experience: Senior Advisor to Arizona Governor Ducey
VP of Outreach, Tucson Hispanic Chamber of Commerce

AZ-07.name: Adelita Grijalva
AZ-07.notNerdReason: B.A. in political science from University of Arizona. Career in nonprofit youth services, education governance, and county government. No STEM or technical background. Policy focus on education and natural resources.
education: B.A. Political Science, University of Arizona
experience: Tucson Unified School District Governing Board, 2002-2022
Director, Pima County Teen Court, 25 years

AZ-08.name: Abraham Hamadeh
AZ-08.notNerdReason: B.A. in political science from Arizona State University and J.D. from University of Arizona. Career as Army intelligence officer and prosecutor. Legal and military background with no STEM credentials or evidence-based policy focus.
education: J.D., University of Arizona College of Law
B.A. Political Science, Arizona State University

AZ-09.name: Paul Gosar
AZ-09.notNerdReason: B.S. and D.D.S. from Creighton University. Practiced dentistry for 21 years and was Arizona Dental Association's Dentist of the Year. Has genuine health science credentials but does not apply scientific methodology to policy. Legislative focus is on public lands and immigration rather than evidence-based governance.
experience: Arizona Dental Association Dentist of the Year
